steps.title: State the no-solution condition
steps.content:
Rearrange $5x+3=rx+7$ by collecting $x$ terms:
$$5x-rx=7-3$$
$$(5-r)x=4$$
For no solution, the coefficient of $x$ must be $0$, producing the false statement $0=4$. That means $5-r=0$, so $r=5$.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $21$: $(5-21)x = 4 \Rightarrow -16x = 4 \Rightarrow x = -\frac{1}{4}$. Exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice B — $8$: $(5-8)x = 4 \Rightarrow -3x = 4 \Rightarrow x = -\frac{4}{3}$. Exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice C — $7$: $(5-7)x = 4 \Rightarrow -2x = 4 \Rightarrow x = -2$. Exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice D — $5$: $(5-5)x = 4 \Rightarrow 0 = 4$. Contradiction — no solution exists. ✓
That matches Choice D — $5$.
